message:
fix(compat): preserve failed transactions and simplify batch execution

Bring the public `fsqlite::compat` surface closer to the behavior callers
actually expect from a rusqlite-style API.

This commit intentionally groups the compat-facing changes that all affect
what application code observes when using the wrapper layer rather than the
lower-level engine directly.

Included changes:
- simplify `execute_batch()` so it stops pretending to be a SQL splitter and
  instead delegates real statement handling to the engine while still treating
  comment-only and delimiter-only input as a no-op;
- preserve `SQLITE_OPEN_READ_ONLY` when translating compat open flags into
  VFS flags, so callers do not silently lose read-only intent on the way down
  the stack;
- change compat transactions to finalize through `&mut self` with explicit
  `finalized` state, which prevents a failed `commit()` from immediately
  falling into `Drop`-driven rollback and discarding a still-recoverable
  transaction;
- extend the compat tests to cover the failed-commit recovery path and update
  the existing transaction call sites to the corrected API shape.

Why this grouping belongs together:
- every file here changes the contract exposed by the `fsqlite` facade rather
  than internal engine behavior;
- the transaction bug fix, the open-flag fix, and the batch simplification all
  matter at the same abstraction boundary and should be auditable together.
